constraints: Keep ordinary movement precise for projectile dodging and aiming
Use rapid predictable counter-steering and braking
Retain a small readable amount of physical momentum
Allow recoil boosts ricochets and weapon effects to influence movement without slippery controls
Keep exact values as playtest variables
Carry boost and ricochet speed briefly into ordinary movement
Let steering and counter-input restore normal limits quickly
Allow bounded route and corner-cut mastery
Do not make momentum tech mandatory for ordinary play
Do not let small enemies constantly interrupt clean boost paths
Keep heavy enemies elites and bosses as blockers or strong deflectors
Do not make baseline boost contact major free damage
Keep offensive ramming as a possible later build direction
Make enemy weight classes legible
Avoid frame-by-frame overlap damage
Keep careless boosting into crowds potentially lethal
Expect players to shoot routes open route around or boost precisely
Allow enemy-specific contact effects such as knockback grabs slows shield disruption or heavy impact
Provide clear damage feedback
Prevent one enemy from repeatedly grinding the player during overlap
Allow different enemies to each inflict a discrete hit
Keep dense crowds dangerous
Use only a tiny aggregation window to prevent frame-order nonsense
Preserve deterministic collision outcomes
Allow a thin formation to be punched through
Reduce speed steering and remaining distance after repeated light-enemy impacts
Let dense crowds absorb the boost and surround reckless players
Keep heavy units as strong stops or deflectors
Communicate momentum loss clearly
Keep direct shove damage low
Allow wall hazard or unit impacts to add modest damage or stagger based on momentum and weight
Do not let ramming replace four-weapon combat
Permit first playable to implement displacement and stagger before detailed impact damage
Cap and deterministically resolve collision chains
Keep all four mounted weapons active during boosts
Do not apply a universal spread or aim-lag penalty
Preserve independent aim and movement directions
Support the fantasy of boosting sideways or backward while all guns fire
Allow only explicit exceptional weapon-specific deviations
Scale burst speed closely with the mech build's movement speed
Make faster builds boost faster and heavier builds slower
Override ordinary weapon drag and mild temporary slows
Allow clearly communicated severe slows immobilization or anchored states to reduce boosting
Classify and expose modifier interactions consistently
Treat boost activation as a hard directional command
Allow instant back-and-forth perpendicular and reverse boost chains
Prioritize arcade responsiveness over strict momentum conservation
Keep D-112 boost-exit carry and decay after the new burst ends
Let prior momentum matter only until another charge is activated

open_questions_added: Baseline enemy projectile and attack-density philosophy
Crowd composition and anti-boost enemy archetypes
Exact boost count recharge timing distance and speed scaling curves
Exact per-enemy contact grace and simultaneous-hit aggregation timing

next_action.instruction: Ask exactly one concise A/B/C decision card selecting whether ordinary combat pressure is based mainly on sparse deliberate aimed attacks, dense bullet-hell-style patterns, or a hybrid of readable aimed threats with bounded authored barrages. Preserve responsive movement, chainable arcade boosts, four simultaneous weapons, dangerous physical crowds, Windows PC first and later Android controls, broad difficulty accessibility, deterministic replay and speedrunning, enemy variety, and visual readability amid heavy player fire. Include movement relevance, aiming and target-priority demands, screen clutter, enemy identity, difficulty scaling, mobile viability, performance and content-authoring cost, and the risk of either making boosts unnecessary or mandatory. Put the recommendation after all A/B/C options at the end of the card. Queue D-121 through D-130 and persist at D-130 unless the user changes the batching rule.
